text: Fill in the blanks about the spinal cord anatomy.
questions: The spinal cord extends from the foramen magnum to the level of *L1/L1-L2* vertebra in adults, and its terminal end is called the *conus medullaris*.
The *cervical/cervical enlargement* supplies the upper limb via the brachial plexus, while the *lumbar/lumbosacral* enlargement supplies the lower limb.
In *Brown-Sequard/Brown-Séquard* syndrome, there is ipsilateral loss of motor function and *proprioception/vibration* with contralateral loss of pain and temperature.
The lateral horn is present only at *T1-L2/T1 to L2* levels and contains *sympathetic/preganglionic sympathetic* neurons.
